steps.title: Read the graph behavior first
steps.content: The line of best fit rises from left to right and passes close to the origin. So the model needs a positive slope and a $y$-intercept near $0$.

steps.title: Reject the horizontal choices
steps.content: Start with the easiest eliminations. Choices C and D are $y=5.2$ and $y=-5.2$, which are horizontal lines. The line of best fit in the graph rises as $x$ increases, so neither horizontal line can match the scatterplot.

steps.title: Reject the negative-slope choice
steps.content: Next test choice B, $y=-1.1x$. This line falls from left to right, which is the opposite of the best-fit line shown in the graph. That eliminates B.

steps.title: Confirm the matching line
steps.content: The only remaining choice is $y=1.1x$. It rises from left to right and passes through the origin, matching the two visual features from the graph. That is choice A.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The best-fit equation is $y=1.1x$, which matches choice A.
